Off Calendar Jul-18-2012 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Confirmation Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Feb-27-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Berstein for Monica Boettcher (Special Administrator) and Honora Boettcher Respondnet) and also appearing for Attorney Charles Triay for Honora Boettcher. Alec Harrington for James Boettcher (Petitioner/Contestant).

Counsel are not aware of any objections. The Court granted both petitions (subject to review of the orders).

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Triay for Nora Boettcher. Alec Harrington for James Boettcher. Bruce Berstein for Monica Boettcher (Special Administrator).

The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial, (order for the same signed in open court).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Contest Appointment Of Executor And Holographic Will
Off Calendar Jul-18-2011 Text Ruling

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jul-18-2011 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Release Original Document For Forensic Examination
Off Calendar Jun-08-2011 Denied

Petition To Contest Appointment Of Executor And Holographic Will
Off Calendar May-02-2011 Continued To Jul-18-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alec Harrington for James Boettcher. Charles Triay for Honora Boettcher. Bruce Bernstein for Honora Boettcher and for Monica Boettcher.

Mr. Triay stated that he was hoping to get discovery back on track. Mr. Harrington stated that he does not expect any objection to the Motion For Release of Will For Forensic Examination and that he plans on modifying the order on the motion to allow all Paties to examine the will. The matter was continued to July 18, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the Partes are going to stipulate to the motion, they are to advise the Probate Examiner.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Contest Appointment Of Executor And Holographic Will
Off Calendar Feb-07-2011 Continued To May-02-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Bernstein for Honora Boettcher and Monica Boettcher. James Boettcher in Prporia Persona.

The Court informed Counsel that the Notice of Hearing needs to be corrected. Mr. Bernstein stated that Monica was presently acting as Administrator, that a Probate Referee was appointed and that the next step was to have the two parcles of property and the coin collection appraised. In addition, he stated that they need to get an estimate of the repairs, that they have a coin collector coming out, that he will re-send discovery and requested a 90-day continuance for setting. The matter was continued to May 2, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and possible trial setting.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Feb-07-2011 Continued To May-02-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-07-2011 Continued To May-02-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Contest Appointment Of Executor And Holographic Will
Off Calendar Dec-13-2010 Continued To Feb-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Triay and Bruce Bornstein for Honora Boettcher. Cynthia Lee for James Boettcher. Monica Boettcher also present.

The Parties stipulated to have Monica Boettcher serve as Special Administrator with general powers and with bond set at $350,000.00. Counsel read the terms of the agreement for the record. Ms. Lee requested a continuance to hire litigation Counsel. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Special Administation was granted (subject to a written order). All remaining matters were continued to February 7, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-16-2010 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Dec-13-2010 Continued To Feb-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-13-2010 Continued To Feb-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Continued To Dec-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Cynthia Lee for James Boettcher (Objector). Bruce Bernstein for Honora Boettcher.

Mr. Bernstein requested his Client be appointed as the Special Administrator stating that there were two properties and that mortgages and insurance needed to be paid. Ms. Lee objected. The matter was continued to December 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard with the competing petition. The Court suggested getting an independent person to serve and to file an ex parte petition for appointment of a Special Administrator.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
